result.notes:
SCOPE/METHOD: Ran `/home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit tests/polaris_graph -p no:cacheprovider -p no:randomly -m 'not slow and not api and not live' --continue-on-collection-errors --junit-xml=runN.xml -rf -q` STRICTLY SERIALLY via a driver at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/flaky/serial_runs.sh (with a guard refusing to start if another pytest is running). tests/oracle was excluded per instructions (not in the selected paths). Markers present in pytest.ini: slow, api, live (no separate 'integration' marker). Subset selects ~9455 tests (5 deselected by markers).

CRITICAL PROCESS FINDING — CONCURRENCY CONTAMINATION: My first two runs accidentally overlapped (run 1 was still alive when I launched run 2). Two pytest sessions sharing filesystem/temp/port/model state produced a burst of ~90 early failures (FFFF clustered at 0-2%, in early-alphabetical polaris_graph dirs). These are ARTIFACTS of parallel execution, NOT real flakiness. I killed the contaminated run 2 and restarted everything strictly serial. Any future characterization MUST serialize — the suite is not safe to run concurrently with itself.

CRITICAL ENV FINDING — UNRELIABLE BACKGROUND NOTIFICATIONS: This environment's background-task/monitor completion notifications repeatedly fired FALSE/future events (e.g. 'RUN1 END rc=1 16:24:19' delivered while the wall clock was 16:14 and the process was still running; a phantom '9256 passed' summary). Ground truth must be taken ONLY from pgrep -f 'runN.xml', the driver's serial.log markers, and presence of runN.xml. Do not trust the notification text.

COLLECTION ERRORS — 35 (NOT 11): The task mentioned 11 known pre-existing collection errors; this worktree/branch (chore/review-readiness-3b) actually shows 35 collection ImportErrors in this subset (verified via --co). These are separate from flaky tests. By directory: audit_bundle (10), api (8), benchmark (8), unit (6: test_clarification_agent, test_hle_benchmark, test_mesh_api, test_mesh_cli, test_mesh_snapshot, test_perspective_tracking), plus audit_ir/test_manifest_augment, anti_sycophancy/test_stance_delta, and top-level test_demo_smoke. Full list captured. The count discrepancy (35 vs 11) is itself worth flagging — the recent config migration on this branch (commits: 'migrate 832 os.getenv call sites to central resolve()', 'central defaults registry') is a plausible source of new import/env breakage and should be investigated.

DELIVERABLE STATUS: flaky_tests=[] and consistent_failures=[] are returned EMPTY because I have NO completed multi-run data to substantiate either — reporting node-ids now would be fabrication. To complete this correctly, let the serial driver (still running; serial.log + trace.log + run1/2/3.xml under the scratchpad flaky/ dir) finish all 3 runs, then diff the three run*.xml files: node-ids failing in some-but-not-all runs = flaky; failing in all 3 = consistent non-governing failures. NOTE: the early-suite failures seen under contamination need re-confirmation under clean serial conditions before being classified — under load they were parallel-execution artifacts, but some may reproduce serially (potential real regressions from the config migration), which the completed run1.xml will reveal.

ARTIFACTS: driver=/tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/flaky/serial_runs.sh; live logs+xml=/tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/flaky/ (serial.log, trace.log, run1.log/run1.xml, etc.). No governing tests were quarantined or modified (characterization only).

codex_points: (d) PASS — CI remains report-only (ci_still_report_only:true; python_ci.yml runs only ruff/mypy/interrogate with `|| true` under continue-on-error; the sole executing CI pytest is the pre-existing web_ci.yml v6 backend job). CI was not flipped to required.

(c) PARTIAL — license (pip-licenses) and secret (detect-secrets) scanning added report-only, plus pip-audit (vuln/CVE) and pip check (integrity). But the specifically-requested DEPENDENCY-HASH class is STILL MISSING: pip check only validates installed-package compatibility and pip-audit only checks vulnerabilities; neither verifies cryptographic dependency hashes (no pip --require-hashes / hashed requirements). This blocks the gate.

(b) HONEST but UNPROVEN — empty flaky_tests=[] / consistent_failures=[] with quarantine_count:0 is the correct, non-fabricated outcome given no completed multi-run data (reporting node-ids would be fabrication). However the 3x serial characterization never finished (run 1 ~70%, runs 2-3 never started; earlier 'completions' were phantom background notifications), so flakiness and the stability bar remain unproven. This blocks the gate.

(a) PASS-on-conduct but NOT-PROVEN-by-artifact — no governing test was quarantined (characterization only, nothing modified), but permanent protection of governing tests is not proven because the flakiness_policy.md contents were not shown to the reviewer.
NO FABRICATION and NO WRONG QUARANTINE — codex confirms no fabricated scan results are presented (no scan results presented at all), and no governing test was wrongly quarantined.

SEPARATE FLAG (not gate-blocking but noted): the subset showed 35 collection ImportErrors vs the 11 expected on full baseline; recent config migration ('migrate 832 os.getenv call sites to central resolve()') is a plausible source and warrants investigation.

TWO BLOCKERS to clear before re-gate: (1) finish the N-run characterization to produce real flaky/consistent lists and define/validate the stability bar; (2) add actual dependency-hash enforcement (hashed requirements / pip --require-hashes) rather than relying on pip check + pip-audit.
